subject: [PATCH RFC v3 4/6] mm/gup: add get_user_page_vma() to fault in a page under a held lock

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called get_user_page_vma() to the Linux kernel's mm/gup.c file. This function is used to fault in a single page under a held lock, either the mmap lock or the per-VMA lock. It simplifies the __get_user_pages() function and allows for more flexible locking behavior. The patch also includes changes to handle specific cases such as VM_IO/VM_PFNMAP VMA and userfaultfd VMA. The goal of this patch is to improve the handling of page faults in the kernel, allowing for more efficient and reliable memory access.
